1. General Terms and Conditions-
1.1 This contract is governed by the General Terms and Conditions, conditions stipulated to this Product/Service as provided in the Marketplace.
1.2 This Contract between the Seller and the Buyer, is for the supply of the Goods and/ or Services, detailed in the schedule above, in accordance with the General Terms and
Conditions (GTC) unless otherwise superseded by Goods / Services specific Special Terms and Conditions (STC) and/ or BID/Reverse Auction Additional Terms and Conditions
(ATC), as applicable
2. Buyer Added Bid Specific Terms and Conditions-
2.1 Generic: 
OPTION CLAUSE: The Purchaser reserves the right to increase or decrease the quantity to be ordered up to 25 percent of bid quantity at the time of placement of contract. The
purchaser also reserves the right to increase the ordered quantity by up to 25% of the contracted  quantity during the currency of the contract at the contracted rates. Bidders
are bound to accept the orders accordingly.
2.2 Scope of Supply: 
Scope of supply (Bid price to include all cost components) : Only supply of Goods
2.3 Certificates: 
Bidder's offer is liable to be rejected if they don't upload any of the certificates / documents sought in the Bid document, ATC and Corrigendum if any.
2.4 Inspection: 
Pre-dispatch inspection at Seller premises (Fee/Charges to be borne by the BUYER):  Before dispatch, the goods will be inspected by Buyer / Consignee or their Authorized
Representative or by Nominated External Inspection Agency (independently or jointly with Buyer or Consignee as decided by the Buyer) at Seller premises (or at designated place
for inspection as declared / communicated by the seller) for their compliance to the contract specifications. Fee/Charges taken by the External inspection Agency and any external
laboratories testing charges shall be borne by the Buyer. For in-house testing, the Sellers will provide necessary facilities free of cost. Seller shall notify the Buyer through e-mail
about readiness of goods for pre-dispatch inspection and Buyer will notify the Seller about the Authorized Representative/ Nominated External Inspection Agency and the date
for testing. The goods would be dispatched to consignee only after clearance in pre-dispatch inspection. Consignee's right of rejection as per GTC in respect of the goods finally
received at his location shall in no way be limited or waived by reason of the goods having previously been inspected, tested and passed by Buyer/ Consignee or its Nominated
External Inspection Agency prior to the goods' shipment. While bidding, the sellers should take into account 7 days for inspection from the date of email offering the goods for
inspection. Any delay in inspection beyond 7 days shall be on the part of the buyer and shall be regularised without Liquidated Damages.
When there is requirement of submission the advance sample, the seller shall inform the buyer promptly through emails about the date of submission of sample to the buyer
nominated Inspection agency.

2.8 Buyer Added Bid Specific ATC: 
Buyer Added text based ATC clauses
Note to Bidders:
1.The manufacturer must be listed under "List of Producers granted EPR Authorization [under E-Waste (Management) Rules, 2016" published by Central Pollution Control Board, 
Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change, Govt. of India.
2.The manufacturer must have BEE Star Label issued by Bureau of Energy Efficiency Voluntary Scheme for Energy Efficiency labelling, May 2006 for at least some of their products
.
3.Pre Dispatch Inspection (PDI) shall be performed at the OEM's factory. The following minimum shall be checked during the same.
a. Compliance of product against tender specifications.
b. The PC assembly area shall be dust proof and protected from ESD.
c. A sample consisting of 5% of the PCs randomly chosen shall be subjected to Environmental chamber test with temperature settings of 0 degrees Celsius and 45 degrees Celsius
.
Item Acceptance Criteria:
1. Material received at site should comply to specifications mentioned in purchase order.
2. Based on Functional Test Report (FTR), item will be accepted and payment will be released.
3. Material should contain back-up media for OS recovery as mentioned in specifications and other accessories supposed to be supplied along with supply of material.
Vendor Evaluation Criteria:
1. Offers from OEM or their authorized Dealer / Distributor are only acceptable. The bidder shall submit valid authorized dealership certificate from the OEM along with the offer 
else offer will be ignored.
2. Desktop PC Systems hardware must have qualified “Windows Hardware Compatibility Program" Certification Process and hardware shall be listed on Windows Compatibility Pr
oduct List. Proof of this shall be given along with offer else offer will be rejected.
3. Vendor has to submit along with offer Technical Literature, User manual, Brochure with specification for offered PC systems. Vendor shall also provide compliance statement of
OEM indicating product offered meets PC systems specification. Offer without submission of these documents will be considered incomplete.
4. Vendor should give declaration of accepting following Terms & Conditions.
Terms & Conditions:
1. Replacement & failure frequency of hard disk: During the warranty period in case any hard disk fails, vendor has to provide replacement without seeking for failed hard disk in 
return. In case of failures are beyond 5%, the entire lot will be rejected during warranty period. Failing to replace with product, meeting the tender specifications, the supplier is li
able for return of entire payment made, with black listing consequences.
2. All PC System supplied shall have exactly same configuration, make and model of motherboard and driver software.
3. OEM of the Desktop PC shall have operational service center at Hyderabad for warranty support. Address and contact details shall be provided for visit by NFC officials for inspe
ction.
4. Bidder should understand time to time Central government guidelines with respect to “Preference to Make in India” products and abide to “Local content” guidelines.
Bidders have to upload Annexure XI and XII(Buyer uploaded ATC document) along with bid duly filled, signed and stamped.
Note: 1) If the bidder is original manufacturer, shall submit local content declaration as per Annexure-XI.
2) If the bidder is dealer/reseller, the following documents shall be submitted.
i) OEM local content certificate as per Annexure-XI in the OEM’s letter head.
ii) Dealership/Authorization certificate establishing relation with OEM.
EMD SUPPORTING DOCUMENTS, IF ANY, SHALL BE SUBMITTED AT THE FOLLOWING ADDRESS WITHIN 5 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF OPENING:
ASSISTANT PURCHASE OFFICER
B1 SECTION
1 ST FLOOR, AADHAR BUILDING
NUCLEAR FUEL COMPLEX, HYDERABD-500062.

d through the system may be uploaded along with the bid as per formet provided (Buyer Uploaded ATC document) for refund of EMD.

This is to notify you that your Bid dated 27-03-2024 for execution of the "Procurement of IT Support 
Equipments for the IGGAARL in Pulivendula, Andhra Pradesh – Package 1", Tender No: 
RySS/NF/IGGAARL/IT -Package 1/2024 for two (2) items and for the Accepted Contract Amount of 
INR. 41,64,481.94/- (Forty-One Lakh Sixty-Four Thousand Four Hundred Eighty-One .Ninety 
Four Indian Rupees only), as corrected and modified follow with the Instructions to Bidders is 
hereby accepted by the undersigned Purchaser.
You are requested to furnish the Performance Security within twenty-eight (28) days in accordance 
with the Conditions of Contract, using for that purpose the Performance Security Form included in 
Section X, Contract Forms, of the Bidding Documents. 
Note: 
1.
Equipment Integrity and Non-Tampering - The equipment supplied by the Supplier to the 
Purchaser shall be in brand new, original condition, and free from any tampering or unauthorized 
modification. Any violation of this clause shall be considered a material breach of the contract, 
entitling the Purchaser to reject the equipment, terminate the contract, and seek compensation 
for any resulting damages or lossses.
2.
Compliance with Schedule of Requirements: The Supplier shall supply the exact models and 
quantities of laptops, desktops, and other equipment as detailed in the Schedule of 
Requirements. No substitutions, alterations, or deviations in specifications, models, or quantities 
shall be permitted unless expressly authorized in writing by the Purchaser. Any failure to comply 
with this requirement shall be considered a material breach of contract, entitling the Purchaser to 
reject the delivery and seek appropriate remedies.
3.
Payment of Bidder: Payment to the Supplier shall be contingent upon the acceptance 
(provisional and final) of the delivered items or equipment. The delivered items shall be deemed 
accepted only upon the submission and approval of an Acceptance Report (provisional and 
final), which shall be prepared by a committee appointed by the Purchaser. The Acceptance 
Report (provisional and final) shall clearly demonstrate the compliance of the delivered items or 
equipment with the technical specifications outlined in the contract agreement.
